summary: Static-analysis-only review of the Italian-language (it) patches in MedicalApp+ (.NET 9). Sandbox has no dotnet SDK / SQL Server / Gemini key, so no build or runtime was possible. Verified the three patch sites plus scanned the whole /app/MedicalApp for any other hardcoded language maps that could still leak English into Italian output.

minor.issue: Culture-aware fallback DateTime.TryParse iterates over {en-US, ro-RO, fr-FR, es-ES, de-DE} but is missing 'it-IT'. Not related to the reported Gemini/interpretation-language bug (this only affects fallback parsing of odd Italian date strings like '27 gennaio 2014'), but is an inconsistency with the newly-added IT language. Recommend adding "it-IT" to the cultures[] array.

MonthLookup dictionary contains English/Romanian/French month names; Italian month names (gennaio, febbraio, marzo, aprile, maggio, giugno, luglio, agosto, settembre, ottobre, novembre, dicembre) are not present. Same scope as above — parser fallback only, does not affect Gemini/OpenAI interpretation-language selection.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Add 'it-IT' to the cultures[] array in Services/SamplingDateParser.cs line 133 and add Italian month names to MonthLookup for parity with other languages. Does NOT fix the reported bug (which is already fixed) — it's a completeness follow-up.
critical_code_review_comments: All 3 targeted patches are syntactically correct and structurally intact:

1) Services/GeminiMedicalInterpretationService.cs lines 30-38: LanguageNames dict contains all 6 entries (en, ro, fr, es, de, it => 'Italian (Italiano)'). Constructor at lines 40-48 is intact (IOptions<GeminiSettings>, IHttpClientFactory, ILogger) — no truncation. line ~128 usage `LanguageNames.TryGetValue(languageCode, out var n) ? n : 'English'` will now correctly resolve 'it' to 'Italian (Italiano)' and inject it into {LANGUAGE_NAME}.
2) Services/MedicalInterpretationService.cs lines 14-22: LanguageNames dict has identical 6-language coverage including ['it'] = 'Italian (Italiano)'. Mirrors Gemini dict exactly.
3) Services/CamBatchService.cs lines 71-79: cultureName switch now includes 'it' => 'it-IT'. Default remains 'en-US'. Try/catch for CultureNotFoundException preserved.

Loc.cs structure verified: 6411 lines total, single _translations dictionary with keys at exact lines 14 (en), 1092 (ro), 2171 (fr), 3229 (es), 4287 (de), 5352 (it). IT dictionary key count = 998, matching EN's 998 — no missing translations detected via key-count parity.

Grep sweep across the entire /app/MedicalApp confirms NO other hardcoded language dictionaries/switches exist that omit 'it'. The only remaining hits for {en/ro/fr/es/de} without 'it' are inside the two already-patched service files (false positives) and inside SamplingDateParser.cs (minor issue reported above).
Program.cs supported cultures list (lines 128-136) already includes new CultureInfo('it'), so ASP.NET localization middleware will accept the Italian cookie.

Root cause of original bug (confirmed): before the patch, when languageCode='it' arrived at Gemini/OpenAI providers, LanguageNames.TryGetValue returned false and fell back to 'English', so the {LANGUAGE_NAME} token in the system prompt was literally 'English' — Gemini dutifully generated English content. B2B batch mode additionally hit the CamBatchService switch which defaulted to 'en-US' culture, so Loc.T() calls inside the batch (PDF summary labels etc.) also served English strings. All three sites are now fixed; the reported bug should be fully resolved once the user rebuilds locally in VS2026.

success_rate.backend: N/A (static analysis only — no build/runtime possible in sandbox)

context_for_next_testing_agent: This .NET 9 app cannot be built or run in the sandbox (no dotnet SDK, no SQL Server, no Gemini key). All runtime testing must happen on the user's local Windows/VS2026 machine. For any future 'add a language' task, the checklist is: (a) Services/GeminiMedicalInterpretationService.cs LanguageNames dict, (b) Services/MedicalInterpretationService.cs LanguageNames dict, (c) Services/CamBatchService.cs SetBatchCultureAsync switch, (d) Program.cs supportedCultures[], (e) Services/Loc.cs new language dict with 998 keys, (f) OPTIONAL Services/SamplingDateParser.cs cultures[] + MonthLookup.
rca of the issue: Bug 1 (B2C mixed: Italian UI titles + English Gemini interpretation): Gemini/OpenAI providers use their own private LanguageNames dictionary to translate the ISO code into a full language name that is injected into the system-prompt placeholder {LANGUAGE_NAME}. When Italian was added in Phase 2 only to Loc.cs (UI strings) but NOT to these two provider-level dictionaries, TryGetValue('it') returned false and the code fell back to 'English', so the LLM system prompt literally said 'Respond in English'. Fixed by adding ['it'] = 'Italian (Italiano)' to both dicts. Bug 2 (B2B batch entirely English): CamBatchService.SetBatchCultureAsync's switch expression mapped only ro/fr/es/de to their full culture strings, defaulting everything else including 'it' to 'en-US'. That set CurrentUICulture to en-US for the whole batch thread, which made every Loc.T(key) inside the batch resolve to English — regardless of the operator's UI setting. Fixed by adding 'it' => 'it-IT'. Both fixes are verified structurally correct; runtime verification must be done by the user in VS2026.
